description: Fix validated bugs and code quality issues in the browser extension: missing CDP event handler causing memory leaks, dropped server notifications due to allowlist gap, confirmation badge count drift, and duplicated utility code.

userStories.title: Handle Network.loadingFailed CDP event in network-capture.ts
userStories.description: As an AI agent using browser_get_network_requests, I want failed network requests (DNS errors, CORS blocks, aborted requests) to be captured and their tracking state cleaned up, so that I can debug network issues and the pendingRequests map does not leak memory.
acceptanceCriteria: A Network.loadingFailed handler is registered in the CDP event listener alongside the existing Network.requestWillBeSent, Network.responseReceived, and Network.loadingFinished handlers
When Network.loadingFailed fires, the corresponding entry is removed from pendingRequests
When Network.loadingFailed fires, the corresponding entry is removed from requestIdToRequest if present
Failed requests are recorded in the requests array with appropriate metadata (status 0, the error reason from the CDP event params, timing info)
The requests array maxRequests cap (shift when full) applies to failed requests the same as successful ones
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts. The CDP event listener starts at line 140 with a switch on params.__event. Currently handles: Network.requestWillBeSent (line 147), Network.responseReceived (line 166), Network.loadingFinished (line 194), Network.webSocketCreated (line 220), Runtime.consoleAPICalled (line 243). Add a case for 'Network.loadingFailed'. The CDP event params include `requestId`, `timestamp`, `type`, `errorText`, and `blockedReason`. Use `requestId` to look up the pending request, build a CapturedRequest with status 0 and the errorText, push to the requests array (respecting maxRequests cap via shift), and delete from both pendingRequests and requestIdToRequest. Follow the same pattern as Network.responseReceived + Network.loadingFinished but combined into one handler since for failed requests there is no separate loading phase.

userStories.title: Add plugins.changed to the offscreen allowlist so server notifications reach the side panel
userStories.description: As a user managing plugins via the MCP server (install/update/remove), I want the side panel to auto-refresh when the server sends a plugins.changed notification, so that I see updated plugin state without manually refreshing.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins.changed is added to PASSTHROUGH_METHODS in platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts
The offscreen document no longer drops incoming plugins.changed WebSocket messages (previously logged 'Dropping message with unknown method: plugins.changed')
The message-router.ts handleServerMessage function forwards plugins.changed to the side panel via the SIDE_PANEL_METHODS set (which already includes plugins.changed at line 92)

No duplicate forwarding occurs — plugins.changed should NOT have a handler in the dispatch table (it is purely a passthrough notification), and the existing explicit forwardToSidePanel calls in handleSyncFull and handlePluginUpdate should remain as-is since they cover the sync.full and plugin.update code paths
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts. Add 'plugins.changed' to the PASSTHROUGH_METHODS array (around line 61-66). This is a one-line fix. The PASSTHROUGH_METHODS are methods that go through to the background message handler but are not dispatched to a specific handler — they are forwarded to the side panel via the SIDE_PANEL_METHODS check in message-router.ts (line 432-434). Since 'plugins.changed' is already in SIDE_PANEL_METHODS (line 92), adding it to PASSTHROUGH_METHODS is sufficient for the message to flow: server → WebSocket → offscreen (passes allowlist) → background (handleServerMessage) → side panel (via SIDE_PANEL_METHODS forward). The MCP server sends this notification from platform/mcp-server/src/extension-handlers.ts at lines 513, 540, and 576 after plugin install/update/remove operations.

userStories.title: Fix confirmation badge count drift on timeout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension badge count to accurately reflect the number of pending confirmation requests, so that the badge does not show a stale count after confirmations time out without a response.
acceptanceCriteria: When a confirmation is removed from pendingConfirmations in the side panel due to timeout, the background is notified to decrement the badge count
The badge count reaches 0 when all confirmations have either been responded to or timed out
The existing clearConfirmationBadge function is called for each timed-out confirmation (not just user-responded ones)
The clearAllConfirmationBadges on disconnect behavior is preserved as a safety net
userStories.notes:
The issue spans two files:

1. platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/hooks/useServerNotifications.ts (lines 42-44): The setTimeout that auto-removes timed-out confirmations calls setPendingConfirmations but does NOT notify the background to decrement the badge. Fix: when the timeout fires and removes a confirmation, send a message to the background (e.g., chrome.runtime.sendMessage with a new message type like 'sp:confirmationTimeout' containing the confirmation id).

2. platform/browser-extension/src/background-message-handlers.ts: Add a handler for the new message type that calls clearConfirmationBadge() from confirmation-badge.ts.

3. platform/browser-extension/src/extension-messages.ts: Add the new message type to the InternalMessage discriminated union.

Also fix the secondary issue: the setTimeout in useServerNotifications is not cleaned up on unmount. Store timeout IDs in a useRef and clear them in the useEffect cleanup. When a confirmation is responded to manually (via handleConfirmationResponse), also clear its timeout to prevent the timeout callback from firing after the user already responded.

userStories.title: Consolidate duplicate requireStringParam utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single requireStringParam function used across the codebase, so that parameter validation is consistent and there is no duplicated code.
acceptanceCriteria: There is exactly one requireStringParam function in the codebase (not two with different error messages)
All callers that previously used dispatch-helpers.ts requireStringParam or browser-commands/helpers.ts requireStringParam import from the same location
The consolidated function uses a consistent error message format
No unused exports remain after consolidation (knip passes)
userStories.notes:
Two files have nearly identical requireStringParam functions:

1. platform/browser-extension/src/dispatch-helpers.ts:39-53 — used by tool-dispatch.ts, resource-prompt-dispatch.ts
   Error: 'Missing or invalid "${paramName}" param (expected non-empty string)'

2. platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/helpers.ts:44-59 — used by cookie-commands.ts, resource-commands.ts, key-press-command.ts, interaction-commands.ts, extension-commands.ts
   Error: 'Missing or invalid ${paramName} parameter'

Both check typeof value !== 'string' || value.length === 0 and send JSONRPC_INVALID_PARAMS error via sendToServer. Pick one location (browser-commands/helpers.ts is the more widely used one), keep it there, remove the other, and update all imports. Use a consistent error message format. The dispatch-helpers.ts version should be removed and its callers (tool-dispatch.ts, resource-prompt-dispatch.ts) updated to import from browser-commands/helpers.ts. If dispatch-helpers.ts becomes empty or has no other exports after this, check if it can be removed entirely (check knip).

userStories.title: Add Chrome API version guards for sidePanel.onOpened/onClosed
userStories.description: As an extension user on an older Chrome version, I want the service worker to start successfully even if chrome.sidePanel.onOpened/onClosed are unavailable, so that the extension degrades gracefully instead of crashing.
acceptanceCriteria: chrome.sidePanel.onOpened.addListener is only called if chrome.sidePanel.onOpened exists
chrome.sidePanel.onClosed.addListener is only called if chrome.sidePanel.onClosed exists
When the APIs are unavailable, the toggle functionality falls back gracefully (the side panel still opens via chrome.sidePanel.open but the toggle-to-close behavior is unavailable)
The existing behavior on Chrome 141+ is unchanged
